title: UNICEF Youth Engagement Internship 2026 in New York (Remote): Fully Remote International Internship Opportunity for Young Professionals
summary: The United Nations Children s Fund is now accepting applications for the Youth Engagement Internship under the Youth Partnerships and Programmes Secretariat of Generation Unlimited . This highly competitive international internship opportunity offers young professionals and graduate students the chance to contribute to global youth empowerment initiatives while working remotely with one of the world’s most respected humanitarian organizations. The internship is hosted under UNICEF’s Global Programme Division and is based in New York Headquarters (NYHQ), although the role will be carried out remotely on a full-time basis for a duration of 26 weeks. UNICEF is recruiting two interns to support key areas within Generation Unlimited’s global youth engagement and innovation ecosystem. The opportunity is ideal for individuals passionate about youth development, entrepreneurship, international development, education, policy, and social impact. Applications close on 08 June 2026 (Eastern Daylight Time). About UNICEF United Nations Children s Fund operates in more than 190 countries and territories worldwide, working to protect children’s rights, improve access to education and healthcare, respond to emergencies, and support young people in reaching their full potential. UNICEF’s mission centers on ensuring every child survives, thrives, learns, and is protected from harm. The organization works closely with governments, communities, civil society organizations, and international partners to create sustainable change for children and youth globally. The organization is recognized internationally for its commitment to: Child protection Education Health and nutrition Youth empowerment Emergency response Social inclusion Gender equality Sustainable development UNICEF also places strong emphasis on diversity, inclusion, innovation, and youth participation in global decision-making processes. What is Generation Unlimited (GenU)? Generation Unlimited is a global partnership initiative launched by UNICEF alongside public and private sector organizations. The initiative focuses on expanding opportunities for young people aged 10–24 by helping them access: Education Skills training Employment Entrepreneurship opportunities Digital inclusion Social participation Generation Unlimited aims to ensure that every young person can transition successfully from learning to earning by the year 2030. The initiative particularly prioritizes vulnerable and marginalized youth groups, including: Girls and young women Youth living in poverty Young people with disabilities Migrants and displaced youth Youth affected by conflicts Youth impacted by climate disasters and emergencies GenU brings together: Governments United Nations agencies Private sector organizations Academic institutions Civil society organizations Youth-led groups Investors Development partners The partnership works to identify, scale, and support innovative youth-centered solutions globally. UNICEF Youth Engagement Internship 2026 Overview Internship Details Category Details Organization UNICEF Programme Generation Unlimited (GenU) Internship Type Full-time Remote Internship Duration 26 weeks Number of Positions 2 Location Remote / New York HQ Functional Areas Communication, Partnerships, Fundraising Application Deadline 08 June 2026 Available Internship Positions UNICEF is offering internship opportunities across two major hubs within Generation Unlimited. 1. Youth Engagement and Entrepreneurship Hub This position focuses on youth participation, entrepreneurship programming, and youth-centered innovation initiatives. Intern responsibilities include: Youth Engagement Supporting meaningful youth engagement strategies Assisting the Young People’s Action Team globally and regionally Supporting implementation of the Youth Centrality Strategy Gathering feedback from marginalized youth groups Helping integrate youth perspectives into programme design Entrepreneurship Programming Assisting entrepreneurship-focused initiatives Supporting mentorship sessions and workshops Helping young entrepreneurs build skills Facilitating connections with strategic partners Programme Support Supporting youth-led programme implementation Aligning projects with GenU strategic priorities Assisting entrepreneurship and youth empowerment programmes Advocacy and Outreach Developing blogs and digital advocacy materials Amplifying youth voices within GenU initiatives Supporting youth participation in advocacy forums 2. Global Solutions Hub This internship focuses on strategic planning, learning-to-earning initiatives, and knowledge-sharing activities. Responsibilities include: Global Solutions Portfolio Supporting resources for UNICEF Country Offices Assisting Learning-to-Earning programming initiatives Supporting GenU programme deployment globally Strategic Planning Assisting the development of GenU’s 2026–2029 strategic plan Supporting country consultations Drafting technical notes and planning documents Contributing to strategic reviews Knowledge Sharing Developing reports and blogs Supporting peer-to-peer learning initiatives Assisting webinars and thought leadership activities Supporting online learning platforms and global youth networks General Responsibilities of Interns All interns will also support broader operational and programmatic activities, including: Collaboration Working closely with GenU programme teams Coordinating with external partners Supporting integrated programme delivery Research and Analysis Conducting research on youth development trends Analyzing entrepreneurship and innovation issues Supporting evidence-based strategy development Administrative Support Organizing meetings Maintaining programme documentation Supporting logistical coordination The internship offers practical experience in: International development Programme management Youth engagement Policy support Global partnerships Advocacy Research Communication Benefits of the UNICEF Internship Selected interns will gain access to numerous professional development opportunities. Key Benefits Include: Hands-on international work experience Opportunity to work with UNICEF and global partners Exposure to international youth development initiatives Professional networking opportunities Experience in global programme implementation Flexible remote working arrangements Leave entitlement Reasonable accommodation support for persons with disabilities Exposure to high-level global policy discussions Skill development in research, advocacy, communication, and strategy The internship also provides a strong foundation for careers in: International development Public policy Youth advocacy Humanitarian work Social innovation Entrepreneurship support Global partnerships Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the UNICEF Youth Engagement Internship 2026, applicants must meet several academic and professional criteria. Applicants Must: Be enrolled in or have recently completed a graduate academic programme Study fields such as: International Relations Political Science Education Business Entrepreneurship Monitoring and Evaluation International Development Other related disciplines Applicants Should Also Have: Interest or experience in youth development and engagement Experience working with adolescents and youth programmes Understanding of humanitarian or development contexts Some volunteer or professional experience in youth-focused work Strong writing and communication skills Ability to prepare concept notes and pitch decks Preferred Skills and Qualifications UNICEF strongly prefers candidates with: Knowledge of research and qualitative analysis Experience with knowledge management Data visualization skills Experience participating in youth advisory bodies Interest in GenU programme areas Fluency in English is required. Knowledge of additional UN languages is considered an advantage, including: Arabic Chinese French Russian Spanish Required Application Documents Applicants must prepare and submit the following documents: Proof of university enrollment or recent graduation Official academic transcript Valid identification card Signed recommendation letter Proof of health insurance coverage for the internship duration UNICEF’s Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion UNICEF emphasizes diversity, equity, and inclusion throughout its recruitment process. The organization welcomes applicants regardless of: Race Ethnicity Gender identity Disability Nationality Religion Socio-economic background Sexual orientation UNICEF also maintains strict safeguarding and protection standards and conducts rigorous background checks on selected candidates. Important Internship Notes Applicants should note the following: The internship does not guarantee future employment with UNICEF. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Health insurance is mandatory. UNICEF may withdraw internship offers if required documentation is not provided. Selected candidates must comply with UNICEF safeguarding policies. Application Deadline Deadline: 08 June 2026 (Eastern Daylight Time) Interested applicants are encouraged to apply early before the deadline. How to Apply Applicants can learn more and apply through the official UNICEF careers platform Apply Here For more information on this internship visit here Discover more internship opportunities on OFY, click here The post UNICEF Youth Engagement Internship 2026 in New York (Remote): Fully Remote International Internship Opportunity for Young Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: WIPO Ethics Office Internship 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland | Paid United Nations Internship for Law and International Relations Graduates
summary: The World Intellectual Property Organization Ethics Office Internship 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland is now open for applications. This highly competitive United Nations internship offers an exceptional opportunity for graduate students and recent graduates in law, ethics, public international law, compliance, and related fields to gain hands-on experience within one of the world’s leading international organizations. Hosted at WIPO headquarters in Geneva, the internship provides successful candidates with practical exposure to ethics policy development, international civil service law, legal research, compliance frameworks, and ethics advisory work within the UN system. The internship is expected to begin on October 1, 2026, with an initial duration of six months and the possibility of renewal up to a maximum of twelve months. The application deadline is June 19, 2026. WIPO is a specialized agency of the United Nations dedicated to promoting innovation and protecting intellectual property globally. Through this internship, selected interns will work directly within the Ethics Office under the supervision of the Chief Ethics Officer and contribute to maintaining integrity, ethical conduct, and accountability across the organization. This opportunity is ideal for aspiring international lawyers, ethics professionals, compliance officers, policy researchers, and individuals interested in careers within the United Nations system. About the WIPO Ethics Office Internship The WIPO Ethics Office Internship Program is designed to help interns: Complement their academic studies with practical international experience Develop legal, analytical, and policy-related skills Gain exposure to ethics and integrity systems within the United Nations Understand international civil service law and governance Build professional networks within international organizations The internship is based entirely on-site in Geneva, Switzerland, and cannot be completed remotely. Interns will work within the Ethics Office under the Sector of the Director General. The Ethics Office plays a crucial role in ensuring that all WIPO personnel uphold the highest standards of integrity, professionalism, and ethical conduct according to: The WIPO Convention WIPO Staff Regulations and Rules The WIPO Code of Ethics Standards of Conduct for the International Civil Service The office also provides confidential ethics advice, develops ethics-related policies, conducts awareness campaigns, and protects personnel from retaliation or reprisals. Internship Location and Duration Internship Location Geneva, Switzerland Expected Start Date October 1, 2026 Internship Duration Initial duration of 6 months Renewable up to 12 months maximum Working Arrangement Full-time 40 hours per week On-site internship at WIPO headquarters Financial Benefits and Internship Support Selected interns will receive several benefits during the internship period. Monthly Stipend Interns will receive: 2,170 Swiss francs per month Additional Benefits The internship package also includes: Medical insurance Annual leave Sick leave benefits Technical supervision and mentorship Professional development within the UN system This stipend is intended for full-time participation in the internship program. What Interns Will Do Interns will support the Ethics Office in legal, policy, communication, and research-related tasks. Main Responsibilities Include 1. Ethics and Policy Research Conduct research for new or revised ethics policies Analyze ethics-related frameworks within the UN system Support comparative legal and policy studies 2. Legal Research and Fact-Finding Collect information and documentation related to ethics cases Conduct jurisprudence and international civil service law research Assist with legal analysis and policy interpretation 3. Ethics Advisory Support Assist in preparing ethics-related guidance Support the development of confidential advisory materials 4. Communication and Awareness Activities Contribute to ethics outreach campaigns Help prepare educational materials and presentations Assist in awareness-raising initiatives 5. Documentation and Administrative Tasks Draft memoranda, reports, and correspondence Prepare PowerPoint presentations Support updates to Ethics Office web pages and intranet content 6. Additional Tasks Perform other related duties assigned by the Chief Ethics Officer Eligibility Criteria WIPO specifies that this internship falls under Category II internships. Applicants must belong to one of the following categories: Eligible Applicants Include Individuals pursuing their first advanced university degree who have completed coursework but not their final thesis Individuals who already hold one advanced degree and are pursuing another university degree Individuals who obtained their most recent advanced university degree less than two years before applying Educational Requirements Essential Requirement Applicants must possess: An advanced degree in law (Master’s level preferred) Preferred academic specializations include: Public international law Organizational ethics Compliance International civil service law Desirable Qualification Specialization in international civil service law Preferred Experience While not mandatory, WIPO values applicants with: Previous experience in international organizations Policy research experience Experience developing communication materials Familiarity with ethics or compliance systems Language Requirements Essential Excellent written and spoken English Desirable Very good knowledge of French Skills and Competencies Required WIPO is seeking candidates with strong professional and interpersonal skills. Key Competencies Include Excellent analytical abilities Strong legal drafting skills Effective written and verbal communication Teamwork and flexibility Integrity and professionalism Sound judgment Confidentiality handling skills Strong organizational capacity Ability to work with sensitive information Technical Skills Applicants should also be proficient in: Microsoft Office applications Zoom Digital collaboration tools Online communication systems Organizational Competencies Interns are expected to demonstrate: Effective communication Integrity Service orientation Innovation and adaptability Team spirit Respect for diversity Result-oriented performance Continuous learning Why This Internship Is Important This WIPO internship is a major opportunity for individuals seeking careers in: International law Human rights Ethics and compliance UN agencies International governance Public policy Diplomacy Global institutions Interns will gain first-hand exposure to how ethics systems function inside a major international organization and develop practical experience that can significantly strengthen future career prospects. How to Apply for the WIPO Ethics Office Internship 2026 Applicants must submit the following documents: Required Documents Motivation letter Curriculum Vitae (CV) Submission Instructions Applications should be sent by email to: erecruit@wipo.int Required Subject Line Ethics Internship position 2026 Important Note For LinkedIn Easy Apply submissions: Combine both the CV and cover letter into one PDF file Applications without both documents will not be considered. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the WIPO Ethics Office Internship 2026 is: June 19, 2026 Candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible. Applicants can learn more about the internship program on here Discover more internship opportunities on OFY here The post WIPO Ethics Office Internship 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland | Paid United Nations Internship for Law and International Relations Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Social Fiction Design Competition 2026: Win USD 10,000 by Imagining a Better World for Young People in 2050
summary: Young writers, dreamers, storytellers, and changemakers from around the world are invited to participate in the Social Fiction Design Competition 2026, a global creative writing competition that challenges participants to imagine a better, fairer, and more sustainable world for young people in the year 2050. Organized by Yunus Center and powered through YY Ventures , this international initiative encourages people everywhere to use the power of imagination and storytelling to envision solutions to today’s social and environmental challenges. The competition is open globally with no age restrictions and no geographical limitations, making it one of the most inclusive international creative writing opportunities currently available. Participants stand a chance to win a grand prize of USD 10,000 while contributing to conversations about the future of humanity, sustainability, innovation, and social transformation. What is the Social Fiction Design Competition? The Social Fiction Design Competition is an international writing competition focused on “Social Fiction” — a form of imaginative storytelling similar to science fiction, but centered on social innovation and human progress rather than futuristic technology alone. The competition invites participants to imagine: What the world could look like in 2050 How young people may live in the future How societies can overcome inequality and injustice What sustainable communities might become How governments, businesses, and communities can work together for positive change The goal is to inspire new ways of thinking about social innovation and encourage people to imagine the world they truly want to build. According to Nobel Peace Laureate Muhammad Yunus : “If we do not imagine, it will not be done. If we imagine, someday it will happen.” This powerful idea forms the foundation of the competition. What is Social Fiction? Social Fiction is a storytelling approach that imagines a future society shaped by positive social transformation. Unlike traditional science fiction, which often focuses heavily on futuristic machines or scientific advancement, Social Fiction focuses on: Human-centered innovation Social justice Sustainability Equality Community transformation Ethical development Inclusive economies Environmental healing The concept encourages writers to creatively explore solutions to real-world problems and imagine societies where communities thrive. The organizers believe that imagination can inspire real-world innovation and policy change. Competition Theme: The Life of a Young Person in 2050 Participants are asked to imagine and describe: “How do you wish to see the life of a young person in the year 2050?” Writers are encouraged to take readers on a journey into a future world that is free from the social and environmental injustices affecting communities today. The competition emphasizes optimism, innovation, and bold thinking. Applicants should imagine a future where systems, institutions, and communities have evolved to create better opportunities and improved quality of life for younger generations. Writing Requirements Participants must submit an original Social Fiction piece between: Minimum: 1,000 words Maximum: 5,000 words The writing should creatively explore a fictional world in 2050 while addressing one or more major social or environmental issues. Suggested Focus Areas Participants may choose to focus on one or several of the following areas: Climate Change Agriculture Technology and Innovation Micro-credit Transport Literature Music Waste Management Circular Economy Tourism WASH (Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene) Employment Sports Health and Well-being Community Development Sustainability Social Equity The organizers encourage participants to think broadly and creatively about how these sectors could evolve positively by 2050. What Should Your Story Include? The competition encourages writers to explain how different stakeholders contribute to building a better future. Stories may include the role of: Governments Educational institutions Corporate organizations Local businesses Community leaders Entrepreneurs Youth movements Civil society organizations Participants should demonstrate how collaboration and innovation help shape a more just and sustainable society. Why This Competition Matters The Social Fiction Design Competition is not just a writing contest. It is a platform for global imagination and social innovation. At a time when the world faces challenges such as: Climate change Rising inequality Unemployment Environmental degradation Mental health concerns Economic instability Social exclusion The competition seeks bold visions of hope and possibility. It encourages people to move beyond criticism and begin imagining actionable futures worth building. Prize Opportunity: Win USD 10,000 One of the major attractions of the competition is the opportunity to win: USD 10,000 The prize recognizes outstanding creativity, originality, optimism, and social imagination. Beyond financial rewards, participants gain international exposure and become part of a global conversation on innovation and sustainable futures. Who Can Participate? One of the strongest features of the Social Fiction Design Competition is its accessibility. There are: No age restrictions No geographical limitations This means anyone from anywhere in the world can participate. Students, writers, professionals, activists, researchers, entrepreneurs, and aspiring storytellers are all encouraged to apply. Can You Apply More Than Once? According to the competition FAQ: Multiple applications are not permitted. Applicants are encouraged to focus on submitting one strong and well-developed Social Fiction piece. Is There an Application Fee? The competition states that there is: No application fee Participation is completely free. Can Teams Apply? The FAQ section indicates that applicants should review the application guidelines regarding team participation. Participants are encouraged to verify current submission rules through the official competition platform. Previous Winners and Finalists The competition has already attracted talented participants from different countries around the world. Previous Winner Amit Kumar Mishra – Nepal Previous Finalists Neha Sabu – India Wan Faizal Mohd – Malaysia Anne Julienne Barcelona – United States Tyfur Rahman – Bangladesh The diversity of past finalists demonstrates the truly international nature of the competition. Why You Should Apply Participating in the Social Fiction Design Competition can help writers and changemakers: Showcase creativity globally Build visibility as a thought leader Contribute to social innovation conversations Inspire real-world solutions Develop storytelling and writing skills Gain recognition for visionary ideas Connect imagination with impact For young innovators, students, activists, and creative thinkers, this is a unique opportunity to influence future thinking through storytelling. How to Apply Applicants are required to: Write an original Social Fiction piece Ensure the submission falls within 1,000–5,000 words Complete the online application form Submit the story through the official competition platform For inquiries: Email: info@yy.ventures WhatsApp: +880 1648-397420 Apply Here For more information on this competition visit here Discover more opportunities on OFY here The post Social Fiction Design Competition 2026: Win USD 10,000 by Imagining a Better World for Young People in 2050 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: BASE Fellows Fall 2026: Fully Remote AI Safety Fellowship Empowering Black Researchers and Leaders Worldwide
summary: The BASE Fellows Fall 2026 programme is now accepting applications from Black researchers, professionals, students, and practitioners interested in advancing their expertise in AI Safety, AI Governance, and AI Security. Designed as a fully remote and part-time fellowship, the programme offers a unique opportunity for members of the Black diaspora to gain hands-on research experience, expert mentorship, and foundational training in some of the world’s most important and rapidly evolving fields in artificial intelligence. Organised by Black in AI Safety and Ethics, the fellowship seeks to increase Black representation and leadership within the global AI ecosystem while ensuring that future AI systems are safe, equitable, accountable, and human-centered. Applications for the BASE Fellows Fall 2026 programme close on 16 June 2026 at 11:59 PM Anywhere on Earth (AOE). About the BASE Fellowship Fall 2026 The BASE Fellowship is a 13-week remote programme running from September 2026 through December 2026 . The initiative is specifically designed to support and develop Black talent in critical AI-related fields that are shaping the future of technology and society. The programme focuses on three major areas: AI Safety AI Governance AI Security Through structured mentorship, collaborative learning, and practical research projects, fellows gain the knowledge and experience necessary to contribute meaningfully to the AI safety ecosystem and emerging governance discussions worldwide. The fellowship aims to ensure that the Black diaspora remains actively involved in shaping the development and regulation of advanced AI systems rather than being excluded from these transformative conversations. Mission of Black in AI Safety and Ethics (BASE) Black in AI Safety and Ethics was established to create a connected and informed Black community within the fields of AI safety, governance, and security. The organisation’s mission includes: Increasing Black representation in AI leadership Supporting Black researchers and practitioners Promoting ethical and equitable AI systems Building a global network of Black AI experts Preventing the marginalisation of Black communities in the age of artificial intelligence Ensuring fairness, accountability, and collective well-being in future AI systems As artificial intelligence increasingly influences economies, governance, communication, employment, and public life, BASE seeks to guarantee that Black voices remain influential in shaping AI policy and technological development. What Fellows Will Gain from the Programme The BASE Fellowship offers substantial professional and academic benefits for selected participants. 1. Structured Expert Mentorship Participants receive direct mentorship from experienced professionals and researchers working in AI safety, governance, and security. This mentorship helps fellows: Refine research skills Develop technical and policy expertise Navigate career opportunities in AI Build long-term professional relationships 2. Hands-On Research Experience Fellows work on real research projects aligned with their selected track and mentor expertise. Participants gain practical experience in: AI risk analysis AI governance frameworks AI safety methodologies Policy development Security considerations for advanced AI systems Responsible AI deployment 3. Foundational Training in AI Safety Ecosystems The fellowship equips fellows with critical foundational knowledge about the rapidly evolving AI landscape. Training areas may include: AI alignment AI ethics Frontier AI risks AI policy and regulation AI governance structures Technical safety research Human-centered AI systems 4. Networking and Community Building Participants join a growing international network of Black researchers, professionals, and AI leaders. This community provides opportunities for: Future collaborations Career advancement Peer support Research partnerships Knowledge exchange 5. Leadership Development The programme helps participants strengthen their leadership capacity in technical, interdisciplinary, and policy-focused AI spaces. Fellows are encouraged to become future leaders capable of influencing the direction of AI development and governance globally. Programme Structure and Timeline The BASE Fellowship Fall 2026 is structured as a flexible part-time programme to accommodate participants’ academic and professional responsibilities. Duration 13 weeks Programme Period September 2026 to December 2026 Format Fully remote Part-time participation Participants are expected to communicate their weekly availability and explain how they intend to balance the fellowship with existing commitments. BASE Fellowship Selection Process The fellowship selection process occurs in multiple stages to ensure that applicants are matched appropriately with mentors and research tracks. Phase 1: Initial Application Review Applicants first submit a comprehensive application including: Resume/CV Educational background Work experience Research interests Motivation statement Professional achievements Track selection Research and leadership experiences Phase 2: Pre-Screening Assessment Applicants who pass the initial review will receive: A coding test for technical tracks, or A work test for non-technical tracks This stage evaluates the candidate’s readiness for the selected fellowship pathway. Phase 3: Mentor Project Matching Candidates who successfully pass the pre-screening stage will then apply to mentor-led projects aligned with their selected track. Mentors review applicants and choose mentees based on compatibility, expertise, and research interests. Phase 4: Final Offers Final fellowship offers are issued directly by the BASE team after mentor selection is completed. Eligibility Requirements The fellowship is open to members of the Black/African diaspora interested in AI safety, governance, and security. Applicants are expected to provide information regarding: Educational background Current employer or affiliation Research interests Technical or policy expertise Leadership and project experiences Time availability Place of residence Optional profile information may also include: LinkedIn Google Scholar ORCID ID GitHub Personal website Who Should Apply? The BASE Fellowship is ideal for: Black researchers in AI-related fields Policy professionals interested in AI governance Students exploring AI safety research AI practitioners and technologists Interdisciplinary researchers Professionals seeking leadership opportunities in ethical AI The fellowship welcomes applicants from technical, policy, and interdisciplinary backgrounds. Why the BASE Fellowship Matters Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming every sector of society, from healthcare and education to governance, security, and employment. However, conversations around AI safety and governance often lack sufficient representation from Black communities globally. The BASE Fellowship seeks to address this gap by creating pathways for Black professionals and researchers to participate actively in shaping the future of AI systems. By supporting Black leadership in AI governance and safety, the programme contributes to building more equitable and inclusive technological futures. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the BASE Fellows Fall 2026 programme is: 16 June 2026 at 11:59 PM Anywhere on Earth (AOE) Interested applicants are encouraged to apply early and prepare all required documents before submission. How to Apply Applicants must complete the official online application form and submit all required materials before the deadline. Official Fellowship Page: BASE Fellows Fall 2026 Application Portal: Apply for the BASE Fellowship 2026 Final Thoughts The BASE Fellows Fall 2026 programme represents a major opportunity for Black professionals, researchers, and students seeking to shape the future of artificial intelligence in ethical, safe, and equitable ways. With expert mentorship, hands-on research opportunities, and a growing global community, the fellowship helps participants position themselves at the forefront of AI safety, governance, and security discussions worldwide. As AI technologies continue to influence humanity’s future, initiatives like the BASE Fellowship are essential for ensuring inclusive leadership and diverse representation within the global AI ecosystem. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post BASE Fellows Fall 2026: Fully Remote AI Safety Fellowship Empowering Black Researchers and Leaders Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: JournalismAI Skills Lab 2026: Free 14-Week Virtual AI Programme for Journalists, Editors, and Media Professionals Worldwide
summary: The 2026 JournalismAI Skills Lab is now officially open for applications, offering media professionals across the globe an exceptional opportunity to deepen their expertise in artificial intelligence, generative AI, large language models (LLMs), and AI agents within journalism and newsroom environments. Organised by JournalismAI at London School of Economics and Political Science and supported by Google News Initiative, this fully virtual and completely free 14-week programme is designed for professionals working at the intersection of journalism and technology. The Skills Lab goes far beyond basic prompt engineering. It provides practical, hands-on instruction that empowers participants to build AI-powered tools, prototypes, and proofs-of-concept that can directly contribute to innovation in newsrooms and media organisations worldwide. Applications are currently open, and interested candidates must apply before 21 June 2026 at 11:59 PM GMT . About the JournalismAI Skills Lab 2026 The JournalismAI Skills Lab is an intensive virtual training programme that equips journalism professionals with practical AI implementation skills. Unlike introductory AI courses, this programme focuses on helping participants actually build and deploy AI-powered solutions relevant to journalism, media workflows, audience engagement, newsroom innovation, and information verification. Participants will learn how to use: Large Language Models (LLMs) Generative AI systems AI agents Python-based AI workflows Journalism-focused AI applications AI prototyping and proof-of-concept development The programme’s primary objective is to help journalists and media professionals become active contributors to AI innovation inside newsrooms rather than passive users of AI technologies. Programme Structure and Duration The 2026 Skills Lab runs for 14 weeks in a live virtual format. Weekly Commitment Participants are expected to dedicate approximately: 7 hours per week Two live sessions weekly Each session lasts two hours Additional self-paced assignments and exercises The programme combines: Live instructor-led classes Hands-on laboratory sessions Strategic product-thinking workshops Independent practical exercises Collaborative learning experiences This blended approach ensures that participants gain both conceptual understanding and practical implementation skills. Regional Cohorts for 2026 This year, the programme will be delivered through two regional cohorts: Africa, Europe, and the Americas Cohort 15 participants selected Asia-Pacific and the Middle East Cohort 10 participants selected This structure enables more tailored collaboration, networking, and learning experiences across similar regional contexts. What Participants Will Learn The JournalismAI Skills Lab curriculum is designed to provide both technical and strategic AI knowledge for media professionals. Core Learning Areas Include: Building AI-powered newsroom tools Developing journalistic AI prototypes Understanding responsible AI practices Creating AI-assisted workflows Product thinking for AI innovation AI implementation in editorial environments Information provenance and AI verification Data-driven journalism enhancement Generative AI applications for audience engagement The programme begins with workshops on product and strategic thinking led by industry leader Sheena Bhalla , Founder of Module Xero. Participants then proceed into 12 weeks of core AI instruction focused on developing practical tools and solutions. Lead Instructors and Industry Experts The programme is led by globally recognised AI and journalism experts. Pedro Henriques Pedro Henriques is Co-founder and CTO of The Newsroom. He previously led teams within LinkedIn’s global Data Science and AI division and has trained more than 500 media professionals across Europe in AI applications for journalism. Jenny Romano Jenny Romano is Co-founder and CEO of The Newsroom. She previously worked at Google and Salesforce and has received international recognition as one of the most influential women in AI and media innovation. Swapneel Mehta Dr. Swapneel Mehta is an AI researcher and co-founder of SimPPL. His work focuses on online transparency, misinformation analysis, and investigative journalism technologies. He has collaborated with journalists across multiple countries and worked with organisations including MIT, Mozilla, Google, and the UNDP. Benefits of the JournalismAI Skills Lab 2026 Participants selected for the programme will gain access to numerous benefits, including: 1. Free Advanced AI Training The programme is fully funded and free for selected participants. 2. Hands-On AI Development Experience Participants will build practical AI-powered prototypes and newsroom tools. 3. Industry-Level Mentorship Learn directly from leading experts in journalism innovation and AI implementation. 4. International Networking Opportunities Collaborate with journalism and media professionals from multiple countries and regions. 5. Product and Strategic Thinking Skills Gain knowledge in designing scalable newsroom AI solutions. 6. Enhanced Career Opportunities Develop highly sought-after AI and media innovation skills. 7. Certificate of Participation Participants receive official recognition upon completion. Who Can Apply? The programme is intended for professionals working in journalism and media-related technology roles. Eligible Applicants Include: Journalists Data journalists Editors Visual editors Copyeditors Product professionals Editorial staff Media innovation professionals Newsroom technology specialists The programme is especially suitable for individuals who already possess some understanding of how AI is currently being used in journalism and newsroom environments. Python and Technical Requirements Applicants with prior Python knowledge are encouraged to apply. However, beginners are also welcome. Candidates without Python experience are encouraged to complete introductory courses before the programme begins. Recommended preparation resources include: Code Academy Google’s Crash Course on Python Learn Python Applicants may need to submit proof of completion if learning Python prior to the course start. Programme Prerequisites To participate successfully, applicants must have: A support letter from their news organisation Stable internet access Availability to commit 7 hours weekly Basic understanding of AI in journalism Prior Python knowledge or willingness to learn before commencement Who Should Not Apply? The Skills Lab is not intended for: Individuals already highly advanced in AI implementation Professionals seeking only incremental improvements to existing sophisticated AI tools Independent journalists who are not affiliated with a news organisation Testimonials from Previous Participants Former participants have praised the programme for its practical approach and transformative impact. Thomas Gröbner – Germany Described the programme as “the gold standard” for AI in the newsroom. Meera K – India Highlighted how the programme simplified complex AI concepts and enabled practical prototype development. Afore Hsieh – Taiwan Praised the supportive environment and beginner-friendly instruction despite coming from a non-technical background. Sara Cooper – Correctiv Explained how the programme provided the confidence and skills needed to lead newsroom AI adoption initiatives. Important Dates Application Deadline 21 June 2026 at 11:59 PM GMT Virtual Information Sessions Interested applicants can join upcoming information sessions on: 27 May 2026 2 June 2026 10 June 2026 Organising Institutions The JournalismAI Skills Lab is organised by: JournalismAI Polis London School of Economics and Political Science The programme is supported by: Google News Initiative How to Apply Applications must be submitted in English through the official online application form. Applicants can also review the Google Doc containing the application questions beforehand to prepare their responses. Official Website: JournalismAI Skills Lab 2026 Why This Opportunity Matters Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming journalism worldwide. Newsrooms increasingly require professionals who can responsibly integrate AI technologies into editorial processes, investigative reporting, audience engagement, and newsroom innovation. The JournalismAI Skills Lab 2026 offers a rare opportunity for journalism professionals to gain practical AI implementation experience directly from leading industry experts in a highly collaborative global environment. For media professionals seeking to future-proof their careers and become leaders in AI-powered journalism, this programme represents one of the most valuable training opportunities available in 2026. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post JournalismAI Skills Lab 2026: Free 14-Week Virtual AI Programme for Journalists, Editors, and Media Professionals Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Free Online Course on Promoting Youth Employment 2026 | Get Certified by ITCILO
summary: Youth unemployment remains one of the biggest global development challenges today. Across many countries, young people continue to face barriers in accessing decent work opportunities, transitioning from education into employment, and securing quality jobs that provide sustainable livelihoods. To help address these challenges, the International Training Centre of the International Labour Organization (ITCILO) has launched a new free self-paced online course titled “Promoting Youth Employment: Challenges, Policies and Emerging Opportunities.” This course provides participants with practical knowledge, policy insights, and emerging labour market trends that can help shape inclusive and sustainable youth employment strategies worldwide. The program is completely free of charge and comes with an official digital certificate of completion upon successfully finishing the course modules and assessment. About the Promoting Youth Employment Course The “Promoting Youth Employment: Challenges, Policies and Emerging Opportunities” course is designed to introduce learners to the International Labour Organization’s (ILO) approach to youth employment. The course combines: Labour market analysis Youth employment policy strategies Practical development approaches Emerging economic opportunities Inclusion and gender equality frameworks Decent work principles Participants will gain valuable insights into how governments, organizations, and development practitioners can better support young people in securing meaningful and sustainable employment opportunities. The course also explores how major global trends such as digitalization, climate change, demographic shifts, and economic transformation are shaping the future of work for young people around the world. Why Youth Employment Matters Youth employment is central to achieving inclusive economic growth and sustainable development. Many young people globally face challenges such as: High unemployment rates Informal employment Limited access to quality jobs Skills mismatches Poor transition from school to work Gender inequality in employment Limited economic opportunities These challenges are becoming even more complex due to: Rapid technological changes Climate-related disruptions Population growth Economic uncertainty Changing labour market demands This course helps participants understand these realities while identifying practical policy solutions that can create better employment pathways for young people. What You Will Learn By the end of the course, participants will be able to: 1. Understand Global Youth Employment Challenges Learners will explore the major factors affecting youth employment globally, including unemployment trends, labour market inequalities, and barriers to decent work. 2. Learn Key Labour Market Concepts The course introduces important labour market concepts used by the International Labour Organization (ILO), helping participants understand employment systems more effectively. 3. Identify Effective Youth Employment Policies Participants will learn practical policy approaches that can support young people in accessing sustainable and decent employment opportunities. 4. Explore Emerging Employment Sectors The course highlights employment opportunities in fast-growing sectors such as: Digital economy Green economy Care economy Creative economy These sectors are increasingly shaping the future global labour market. 5. Understand Inclusion and Gender Equality Special attention is given to: Gender equality Social inclusion Decent work standards Equitable labour market participation Participants will learn why inclusive employment strategies are essential for sustainable development. Course Modules The course is divided into three structured modules designed to progressively build understanding and practical knowledge. Module 1 – Understanding the Youth Employment Challenge This module examines the current realities of youth unemployment and labour market transitions worldwide. Module 2 – Promoting Youth Employment: Policies and Solutions Participants explore evidence-based policy approaches and practical solutions that support youth employment. Module 3 – Promoting Youth Employment in Emerging Sectors This section focuses on future-oriented employment opportunities in sectors driving economic transformation and sustainability. Each module includes: Conceptual explanations Real-world examples Interactive exercises Knowledge assessments Reflection activities Course Duration and Learning Format The course is fully self-guided, allowing participants to study at their own pace from anywhere in the world. Duration Approximately 4–5 hours total learning time Learning Format 100% online Self-paced Flexible learning schedule This makes it ideal for: Working professionals Government officials Researchers Students Development practitioners Languages Available The course is accessible in multiple languages, making it available to a broad international audience. Available languages include: English French Spanish Portuguese Certification Participants who successfully complete the modules and pass the short assessment test will receive an official ITCILO digital certificate of completion. This certificate can help participants: Strengthen their professional profile Demonstrate expertise in youth employment policy Enhance career opportunities Showcase commitment to sustainable development and labour market inclusion Who Should Take This Course? This course is particularly valuable for: Government policymakers Employment officers Youth program coordinators NGO professionals Development practitioners Labour market researchers Academics Trainers and educators International organization staff Students interested in labour and development issues Anyone interested in understanding the future of youth employment and sustainable labour market solutions can benefit from this course. Benefits of Joining the Course Participants can gain several important benefits, including: Free access to expert knowledge Flexible self-paced learning Internationally recognized certificate Practical labour market insights Understanding of emerging job sectors Knowledge of youth employment policy frameworks Enhanced professional development Improved understanding of inclusion and gender equality in employment How to Enroll Click here for more and to apply Since the course is self-paced, participants can start learning immediately after registration. Final Thoughts As the future of work continues to evolve rapidly, understanding youth employment challenges and opportunities has become increasingly important for governments, organizations, and development professionals worldwide. The “Promoting Youth Employment: Challenges, Policies and Emerging Opportunities” course offers a valuable opportunity to gain practical skills and insights from the International Labour Organization’s approach to decent work, inclusion, and sustainable labour market development. With free enrollment, multilingual access, flexible learning, and certification included, this course is an excellent opportunity for professionals and learners who want to contribute to creating better employment opportunities for young people globally. Click here for more global training opportunities. The post Free Online Course on Promoting Youth Employment 2026 | Get Certified by ITCILO appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
